title: Why Gendo never talk to Asuka
display_title: Why Gendo never talk to Asuka
wikitext:
[]: Why Gendo never talk to Asuka?             Quote Post by Wildchild » Thu Jan 20, 2005 1:58 pm I know that this question is a bit silly, but this is a thing that i keep in my mind since i watched this anime... He talks to Rei and Shinji but never talks to Asuka! Maybe he hates her who know... Because in 22 episode when Shiji ask him to save her he simply say "I Don't Care"... People tell me what you think about this. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Jan 20, 2005 2:47 pm Asuka was expendable to him, just another pawn inhis game. Her life had no meaning to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 20, 2005 3:11 pm Shinji was the child of his body, and Rei the child of his making. Asuka was just another NERV staff member - and how often does the CEO speak to a shop floor worker? And then Gendou himself is not exactly the most garrulous of characters. Ep #22 "I don't care" - at that point, the last thing he is going to do is risk EVA-01, given her pivotal position in his master plan. The pilots are valuable to him only in that they can serve his hidden agenda, as opposed to SEELE's. I don't think that he would have cared to save Shinji, were positions reversed.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Jan 20, 2005 6:15 pm DatDude wrote: Asuka was expendable to him, just another pawn inhis game. Her life had no meaning to him.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Jan 20, 2005 7:17 pm >takes a bow< Thankyou Thankyou

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Fri Jan 21, 2005 7:33 am Well, there is also lack of love for Shinji from Gendo, we know this, because in the first episode he refers to Shinji as a spare.. now if his own son is just a spare to him than obviously Asuka wil mean less than nothing.. So to that extent, what does Shinji mean to him apart from the fact that he is his son and a Eva pilot? :? "Asuka was expendable to him, just another pawn in his game. Her life had no meaning to him." So yea I guess you hit the nail with that statement 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Wildchild » Fri Jan 21, 2005 8:03 am "Asuka was expendable to him, just another pawn in his game. Her life had no meaning to him." You are right! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Fri Jan 21, 2005 8:06 am Wildchild wrote: You are right! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Jan 21, 2005 10:48 am Titus wrote: So to that extent, what does Shinji mean to him apart from the fact that he is his son and a Eva pilot?

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Fri Jan 21, 2005 11:21 am I truely think Gendo is secretly doing it with Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Jan 21, 2005 11:35 am Nah, she shot him down because he was too old. So he had to back to making out with the clones from the tank.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Fri Jan 21, 2005 11:39 am I, myself, wouldn't mind doing that XD

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Jan 21, 2005 3:07 pm max3000 wrote: I truely think Gendo is secretly doing it with Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Fri Jan 21, 2005 5:19 pm Hunter21 wrote: Nah, she shot him down because he was too old. So he had to back to making out with the clones from the tank.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Fri Jan 21, 2005 6:00 pm I meant a Rei clone.

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Fri Jan 21, 2005 6:02 pm max3000 wrote: I meant a Rei clone.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 22, 2005 1:39 am It is a bit odd to think that Gendo and Asuka don't have a single exchange to think of. But then again, in terms of the character relationships, there is none. The relationship is the impetus for the various exchanges the characters have. Shinji is Gendo's son, and Rei is a clone of his dead wife. Asuka has no connection to Gendo, and as Misato delegates between Gendo and the pilots, he is never in the position to speak to Asuka. He only commanded one sortie, and that was only when Misato was incompasitated. In that instance, he gave Asuka an order, and I believe that may have been the only time he ever spoke directly to her. Strange, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by mike » Sun Jan 23, 2005 8:06 am You could also count in the brief interaction in the 5th stage of the 4th volume of Sadamoto's manga. Asuka clearly tries to impress Gendo but he just walks past her and Shinji without saying anything important. That's actually the only time I've ever seen Gendo actually say something which wasn't an order or something important. It's as if he almost indulged in a bit of chit-chat.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jan 23, 2005 11:04 am mike wrote: You could also count in the brief interaction in the 5th stage of the 4th volume of Sadamoto's manga. Asuka clearly tries to impress Gendo but he just walks past her and Shinji without saying anything important. That's actually the only time I've ever seen Gendo actually say something which wasn't an order or something important. It's as if he almost indulged in a bit of chit-chat.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 23, 2005 1:03 pm Kind of OOC. The great Gendo-sama is clearly beyond something as frivolous as small talk.
